# QuickCode Generated Project — AI Agent Guide

> **Read this first.** This repo is generated by [QuickCode](https://quickcode.net).  
> Replace `{Project}` with the PascalCase site name (e.g. `MyShop`) and `{Module}` with the module folder name (e.g. `OrderModule`).

---

## 1. File ownership (critical)

### QuickCode-owned — NEVER edit, create, or delete manually

Regenerated on `quickcode generate`:

| Pattern | What it is |
|---------|------------|
| `*.g.cs` | DBML/DSL generated C# (entities, repos, controllers, handlers, DTOs, …) |
| `*.qc.cs` | QuickCode template scaffold (Program, DI bootstrap, …) |
| `*.g.sql` | Generated SQL scripts |
| `Views/Generated/**` | Generated portal Razor views |

### User-owned — ALWAYS put new work here

**Any plain `.cs` / `.cshtml` file that is NOT in the table above.**

- Regen **never overwrites** existing user `.cs` files.
- Regen **never deletes** user files.
- Use **new files** with normal names (`OrderPricingService.cs`, not `*.g.cs` / `*.qc.cs`).

---

## 2. Where to put new code (decision tree)

```
Need new table / API endpoint / query / DTO from schema?
  → Edit src/dbml_files/{Module}.dbml
  → Run: quickcode {project} generate
  → Do NOT hand-write *Controller.g.cs or *Repository.g.cs

Extend a generated class (repository, controller, entity)?
  → Same layer folder as the .g.cs file
  → New file: {Name}Extensions.cs or {Name}Partial.cs
  → declare: public partial class SameNameAsGenerated { ... }

Register custom services (DI)?
  → Edit SiteServiceRegistration.cs in the host project (Api / Portal / Gateway / EventListener)
  → Add registrations inside AddSiteCustomizations() — never edit Program.qc.cs or Startup.qc.cs

New application / business logic (service, handler, validator)?
  → src/Modules/{Module}/Core/QuickCode.{Project}.{Module}.Application/
     Features/…  (CQRS)  or  Services/…  (Service pattern)

New domain type (not from DBML)?
  → src/Modules/{Module}/Core/QuickCode.{Project}.{Module}.Domain/

Custom data access (not from DBML)?
  → src/Modules/{Module}/Infrastructure/QuickCode.{Project}.{Module}.Persistence/
     Repositories/…  or  Stores/

Custom REST API (controller not tied to a DBML table)?
  → src/Modules/{Module}/Presentation/QuickCode.{Project}.{Module}.Api/
     Controllers/MyFeatureController.cs

Custom portal UI (Razor)?
  → src/Presentation/QuickCode.{Project}.Portal/Views/…
  → NEVER under Views/Generated/

Portal controller / helper for a module?
  → src/Presentation/QuickCode.{Project}.Portal/Controllers/{Module}/
  → src/Presentation/QuickCode.{Project}.Portal/Helpers/

Gateway (routing, auth, Kafka, workflows)?
  → src/Presentation/QuickCode.{Project}.Gateway/

Shared across modules?
  → src/Common/QuickCode.{Project}.Common/

Background / event processing?
  → src/Services/QuickCode.{Project}.EventListenerService/

Tests for user code?
  → Mirror path under *.Tests projects next to the layer being tested
```

---

## 3. Folder map (copy-paste paths)

```
src/
├── dbml_files/                          ← schema source; edit then regenerate
├── Common/QuickCode.{Project}.Common/   ← cross-cutting shared code
├── Modules/{Module}/
│   ├── Core/
│   │   ├── QuickCode.{Project}.{Module}.Application/   ← business logic, handlers, DTOs (user .cs OK)
│   │   └── QuickCode.{Project}.{Module}.Domain/        ← domain models (entities are *.g.cs)
│   ├── Infrastructure/
│   │   └── QuickCode.{Project}.{Module}.Persistence/   ← repos (*.g.cs), custom stores (user .cs OK)
│   └── Presentation/
│       └── QuickCode.{Project}.{Module}.Api/           ← API (*.g.cs controllers), custom controllers (user .cs OK)
├── Presentation/
│   ├── QuickCode.{Project}.Gateway/
│   └── QuickCode.{Project}.Portal/
│       ├── Controllers/
│       ├── Helpers/
│       └── Views/
│           ├── Generated/               ← QuickCode-owned — DO NOT EDIT
│           ├── Home/                      ← user custom views OK
│           ├── Shared/
│           └── {Module}/                  ← user custom views OK
└── Services/QuickCode.{Project}.EventListenerService/
```

---

## 4. Examples

### Extend generated repository

```csharp
// File: src/Modules/OrderModule/Infrastructure/QuickCode.MyShop.OrderModule.Persistence/
//       Repositories/Order/OrderRepositoryExtensions.cs
namespace QuickCode.MyShop.OrderModule.Persistence.Repositories;

public partial class OrderRepository
{
    public async Task<decimal> CalculateTotalAsync(int orderId, CancellationToken ct = default)
    {
        // custom logic
    }
}
```

Do **not** edit `OrderRepository.g.cs`.

### Register a custom service (DI)

Each host project includes a user-owned `SiteServiceRegistration.cs` (plain `.cs`, never overwritten on regen).
QuickCode calls `AddSiteCustomizations()` from `Program.qc.cs` or `Startup.qc.cs`.

```csharp
// File: src/Modules/OrderModule/Presentation/QuickCode.MyShop.OrderModule.Api/SiteServiceRegistration.cs
public static class SiteServiceRegistration
{
    public static IServiceCollection AddSiteCustomizations(this IServiceCollection services)
    {
        services.AddScoped<IPricingService, PricingService>();
        return services;
    }
}
```

Do **not** edit `Program.qc.cs` for DI.

### New application service

```csharp
// File: src/Modules/OrderModule/Core/QuickCode.MyShop.OrderModule.Application/
//       Services/Pricing/PricingService.cs
```

### Custom portal view

```
src/Presentation/QuickCode.MyShop.Portal/Views/OrderModule/Reports/Summary.cshtml
```

Not `Views/Generated/…`.

### New API from schema

Edit `src/dbml_files/OrderModule.dbml` → add table/query Note → `quickcode my-shop generate`.

---

## 5. Hooks & CI

- Pre-commit: `.githooks/pre-commit` — run `git config core.hooksPath .githooks`
- CI blocks manual edits to `*.g.cs`, `*.qc.cs`, `*.g.sql`, `Views/Generated/**`

---

## 6. Legacy note

Older regens may leave duplicate scaffold `Program.cs` alongside `Program.qc.cs`. Keep `Program.qc.cs`, delete duplicate scaffold `.cs`, put custom startup in a new user `.cs` (e.g. `ProgramExtensions.cs` with `partial class Program` or extension methods called from `Program.qc.cs`).
